name: Naringenin/flavonoid sensor (FdeR + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: Detects naringenin and related flavonoids (dietary and nutraceutical compounds). The Marionette FdeR sensor gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the food-grade GRAS chassis Lactococcus lactis.

input.detects: Naringenin/flavonoids in food, beverages, and fermentation

sensing.sensorMechanism: FdeR (LysR family) activates its promoter on binding naringenin.
sensing.signalFlow: Naringenin -> FdeR activates PfdeA -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies a fluorescent reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence rises with naringenin.

parts.name: FdeR regulator
parts.source: Marionette sensor (Meyer et al. 2019); Herbaspirillum fde system

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak reporter promoter

performance.notes: FdeR is a characterised orthogonal Marionette sensor; integrated CRISPRa device is a design template (requires porting to L. lactis).

safety.biocontainment: thyA-based containment in L. lactis; food-grade host.
safety.notes: GRAS / food-grade chassis; suited to food and nutraceutical screening.

buildSteps.title: Port + design
buildSteps.description: Codon-optimise FdeR/PfdeA for L. lactis; design the CRISPRa sgRNA.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: FdeR + PfdeA -> sgRNA. TU2: dCas9-omega. TU3: weak promoter -> mCherry. NICE vectors.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Add food sample + naringenin standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the Marionette FdeR naringenin sensor with L. lactis CRISPRa
partsSources: Meyer et al. 2019, Nat. Chem. Biol. (Marionette sensors, FdeR)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
